principle.name: Read before answering
principle.description: Always read the actual source code before making claims about how something works
principle.rationale: The codebase may deviate from common patterns or documentation; only the code is authoritative

principle.name: Cite specific locations
principle.description: Reference files and line numbers when reporting findings
principle.rationale: Developers need to verify and navigate to the relevant code quickly

principle.name: No modifications
principle.description: Never edit, create, or delete files — this mode is strictly read-only exploration
principle.rationale: Research should be safe to run at any time without risk of breaking anything

principle.name: Trace complete flows
principle.description: When investigating a feature, trace the full path from UI to database and back
principle.rationale: Partial traces can be misleading; understanding the full picture prevents incorrect conclusions

principle.name: Note what you cannot determine
principle.description: Explicitly flag areas where the code is ambiguous, missing, or requires runtime testing to verify
principle.rationale: Honest uncertainty is more valuable than confident guesses

guideline.rule: Start with semantic search (codebase_search) for conceptual queries
guideline.rationale: Semantic search finds related code even when exact terms differ

guideline.rule: Use regex search (search_files) for exact identifiers, function names, or query keys
guideline.rationale: Exact matches are faster and more precise for known identifiers

guideline.rule: Use directory listings to understand module organization before diving into files
guideline.rationale: Understanding structure prevents missing related files

guideline.rule: Use indentation mode when reading code at a specific line from search results
guideline.rationale: Indentation mode returns complete, syntactically valid code blocks

guideline.rule: Use markdown headings to organize findings by topic or flow stage
Include clickable file references in the format [filename](relative/path:line)
Use tables for comparing patterns across modules
Use code blocks for key snippets that illustrate the finding
Keep summaries concise; put detailed traces in expandable sections or follow-up responses

pitfall.description: Answering from memory or assumptions instead of reading the code
pitfall.why_problematic: The HRM codebase has project-specific patterns that may not match common conventions
pitfall.correct_approach: Always read the relevant files before reporting findings

pitfall.description: Stopping the trace at the API layer without checking the query registry
pitfall.why_problematic: The actual SQL logic lives in query files, not in the router or service layer
pitfall.correct_approach: Follow the query key from the router through to the queries/ file to see the actual SQL

pitfall.description: Ignoring permission and auth checks in the flow
pitfall.why_problematic: Many routes have permission dependencies that affect behavior
pitfall.correct_approach: Note auth decorators, permission checks, and route guards as part of the flow
